action.reasoning: Observation: current failures are entirely due to checkpoint-architecture compatibility. The command output pinpoints two concrete bugs: legacy checkpoints with horizon_mixer use Conv1d but forward passes a 4D tensor, and fixed_retrain/end-conv checkpoints have post as Conv2d [96,96,1,1] plus end_conv_2 temporal kernel width 12. Analysis: with only 5 iterations left, retraining is dominated by fixing exact backward-compatible loading and immediately validating all saved checkpoints on val using the same predict-time path. The safest path is to rewrite model.py to support three exact families: legacy MLP head with optional Conv1d horizon_mixer applied on [B*N,H], end-conv family with optional Conv2d post and variable end_conv_2 kernel width, and the per-node residual family. Then run a validation sweep and local predict smoke test, and submit the best validated checkpoint. Decision: patch model.py comprehensively, add a small validation scorer using the same loader path, run it plus local predict, then submit the best checkpoint found.
action.command: python val_search.py && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ema_weighted.pt --output local_test/output.json
